title: Ping An puts its full AI stack on display at WAIC: 90M-user family doctor, 100% claims AI, 251M-customer assistant
subtitle: China's largest private insurer shows what mid-cycle enterprise AI looks like at scale — a national-scale healthcare agent, full-coverage claims automation, and a cross-group financial assistant.
author: AESOP AI Engine
body: Ping An Insurance used the closing days of the 2026 World Artificial Intelligence Conference in Shanghai to lay out the current state of its AI deployment across healthcare, insurance, and payments. The scale is the story. AI Family Doctor, the group's consumer health agent, now serves 90 million monthly active users. The underlying AI Doctor system supports diagnostic reasoning across more than 11,300 diseases and treats close to 12 million users per year. Ping An Property & Casualty reports 100 percent AI coverage across its core business scenarios, with an 80 percent lift in overall operational efficiency versus pre-AI baselines. A new group-wide financial AI assistant, Express Service, is now rolled out to 251 million customers across insurance, banking, securities, and healthcare.

The company also announced its first "disease-specific AI product portfolio," aimed at patients with cancer and other critical illnesses. It bundles three pieces: a disease-prediction service that flags high-risk individuals early, disease-specific insurance products covering people who already have a chronic diagnosis, and the Peking University Healthcare AI Disease Manager, which coordinates a patient's full care pathway across physicians, hospitals, and rehabilitation providers. This is a shift from single-purpose diagnostic tools toward vertically integrated care management, where the same operator holds the AI, the insurance, and the coordination layer.

What Ping An is showing at WAIC is a different vintage of AI story from most Western coverage right now. It is not a model release or a benchmark. It is a large financial-services conglomerate reporting production numbers on AI systems it has been running for years, in categories — insurance underwriting, claims adjudication, primary-care triage — where every 1 percent of efficiency compounds against a very large book of business. The 100 percent claims-AI coverage number is worth pausing on. Very few Western insurers of comparable size can say that.

For learners: enterprise AI in 2026 is less about which model is smartest and more about who can wire a lot of ordinary AI into the operations of a real business, at scale, without breaking regulated processes. That is a slower, less glamorous discipline than model research, but it is where a lot of the actual value is being captured. If you are a student thinking about a career track, notice the roles that sit between a model and a compliant production workflow. Those roles multiplied inside Ping An long before they multiplied at most Western competitors, and they are what a headline like this actually rests on.
